Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Needs better documentation with regards to ZFS MMP/spl_hostid

Patch Set 5:

If this patch is to be refreshed, please do not use "MMP" to describe this feature, since it is not the same thing as MMP on ldiskfs, and there are plans to implement real MMP for ZFS at some point, so this just adds confusion.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

I agree, patch contents need to be reworked to not call this MMP.

Patch Set 5:

Nathaniel, any chance to refresh this patch?  I'm a bit surprised that this fell by the wayside and didn't make it into 2.8, since it represents a serious danger to Lustre+ZFS users.

Ping reviewers.  It would be good to get this landed to avoid dangerous actions by new users.
Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This break important developer usecase for local mount:

[root@centos6-0 tests]# sh llmount.sh
Stopping clients: centos6-0.localnet /mnt/lustre (opts:)
Stopping clients: centos6-0.localnet /mnt/lustre2 (opts:)
cannot run remote command on set_hostid with no_dsh

So this case needs to be properly handled before this patch could be accepted.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Rebased, fixed merge, and addressed Oleg's local mount issue.

commit.subject: LU-7134 utils: Ensure hostid set for ZFS during mkfs
commit.message:
LU-7134 utils: Ensure hostid set for ZFS during mkfs

Ensure import protection in ZFS.  spl_hostid must be set to a
non-zero value for it to work correctly.  Error out during mkfs and
tunefs unless override is passed.  Also print error during mount.

Ensure hostid is set in conf-sanity.sh

Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Change-Id: If37318c20e4502a643992e6bc21ac4bc4ad20ed7
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16611
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
